name: Trump v. United States
summary: Presidential immunity from criminal prosecution for official acts within constitutional authority; established framework for distinguishing official from private conduct.
holding: Presidents have absolute immunity from criminal prosecution for core constitutional powers; presumptive immunity for official acts within outer perimeter of official responsibility; no immunity for unofficial acts.
relevance_to_audit: Establishes modern framework for executive authority and immunity; relevant to analyzing scope of executive agency authority and official conduct boundaries.
